source_pr.title: executor: make IndexHashJoin support keeping the outer order
source_pr.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/community/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.
-->

**NOTE**:
For testing the correctness, I change the execution logic that IndexJoin and IndexMergeJoin both execute IndexHashJoin actually.

### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->
Make the IndexHashJoin support keeping the result as the outer order.

### What is changed and how it works?
Add a keepOuterOrder attribute for IndexHashJoin, outerWorker, and IndexHashJoinTask, when keepOuterOrder is true, a task will be sent to the channels maintained in the main thread and inner workers separately. And the result will be fetched from task.resultCh one by one.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

Exists tests.

Code changes

 - Has exported function/method change
 - Has exported variable/fields change


Side effects

N/A

Related changes

N/A

Release note

N/A

symptoms: tk.MustQuery("explain select /*+ TIDB_INLJ(s) */ * from t left join s on t.a=s.a order by t.pk").Check(testkit.Rows( (executor/join_test.go)
failure_signature: tk.MustQuery("explain select /*+ TIDB_INLJ(s) */ * from t left join s on t.a=s.a order by t.pk").Check(testkit.Rows( (executor/join_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because result ordering depended on async execution details: IndexHashJoin runs inner workers concurrently and previously did not reliably preserve the required outer-row order when `ORDER BY` was present. This can lead to intermittent order mismatches depending on scheduling. The PR stabilizes it by adding a keep-outer-order execution path for IndexHashJoin (and regression tests asserting `keep order:true` in EXPLAIN).
fix_pattern: When query semantics require a stable output order, ensure concurrent execution paths preserve/merge results deterministically (e.g. keep-outer-order mode) instead of relying on goroutine scheduling.

evidence: evidence: tk.MustQuery("explain select /*+ TIDB_INLJ(s) */ * from t left join s on t.a=s.a order by t.pk").Check(testkit.Rows( (executor/join_test.go)
